name: Orange Butter Cakes
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Orange Peel; candied, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Generously butter 36, 2-1/2 inch tartlet tins.
3. In a bowl, combine flour, baking powder, and salt; set aside.
4. In a large mixing bowl, beat together the butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
5. Blend in vanilla extract and eggs, then add Grand Marnier 1 tablespoon at a time, beating after each addition.
6. Gradually stir in the flour mixture in half-cup increments, blending after each addition.
7. Stir in the orange peel.
8. Fill buttered tins about 2/3 full with batter. Use fingertips to press dough into tins so it conforms to the sides and level off the top of the dough.
9. Arrange tins on baking sheets and bake for 15-18 minutes, or until golden.
10. Let cakes rest in tins for 3 minutes before turning them out onto a rack over waxed paper.
11. If necessary, use the tip of a knife to loosen cakes from the molds.
12. Leave cakes upside down; the bottoms will become the tops of the cakes.
Glaze:
1. While the cakes are baking, prepare the glaze by combining powdered sugar and half the Grand Marnier in a bowl.
2. Add enough additional Grand Marnier until it forms a smooth, creamy glaze of medium consistency.
3. Spoon the glaze over warm cakes, covering the tops and allowing it to drizzle down the sides.
4. Sprinkle a few strips of candied orange peel on top of glazed cakes.
5. Allow the cakes to cool completely on racks before serving. Store in an airtight container at room temperature for 3-4 days or freeze for longer storage.
